marketName: Other Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing
coreJobStatement: Manufacture and deploy water-using commercial machinery that cleans, treats, purifies, or dispenses aqueous fluids for commercial and institutional operations [SRC: VN_other_commercial_and_service_industry_ma]
lPath: L7 Commercial Water-Using Machinery Ecosystem > L6.2 Fluid Circuit & Distribution > L5.2.4 Flow Sensing & Measurement > L4.2.4.1 Ultrasonic Flow Sensor

segments.name: High-volume vended laundry
segments.characteristics: Self-service laundromat; 10+ machines running 8–14 cycles/day; pay-per-use billing requires per-cycle water metering; harshest consumer abuse; highest downtime cost
segments.targetabilityReason: pay-per-use billing demands metered consumption; no operator present to detect faults
segments.circumstance: Self-service laundromat; 10+ machines running 8–14 cycles/day; pay-per-use billing requires per-cycle water metering; harshest consumer abuse; highest downtime cost
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — pay-per-use billing demands metered consumption; no operator present to detect faults

segments.name: On-premises institutional laundry
segments.characteristics: Hotel, hospital, prison, nursing home; captive use; regulatory hygiene standards (HLAC, TRSA); water audit and sustainability reporting required; dedicated operator on shift
segments.targetabilityReason: compliance documentation and water-use benchmarking (10–15 L/kg target) drive sensor accuracy requirements [SRC: web_search — Girbau, Ecolab]
segments.circumstance: Hotel, hospital, prison, nursing home; captive use; regulatory hygiene standards (HLAC, TRSA); water audit and sustainability reporting required; dedicated operator on shift
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — compliance documentation and water-use benchmarking (10–15 L/kg target) drive sensor accuracy requirements [SRC: web_search — Girbau, Ecolab]

segments.name: Express tunnel car wash
segments.characteristics: 100–400 vehicles/day; water recycling mandatory in many jurisdictions; speed critical (3–5 min/vehicle); continuous high-volume operation; 50–80% water reclaim typical [SRC: web_search — NCS, EPA WaterSense]
segments.targetabilityReason: recycled water quality variability (TDS, turbidity) stresses sensor robustness
segments.circumstance: 100–400 vehicles/day; water recycling mandatory in many jurisdictions; speed critical (3–5 min/vehicle); continuous high-volume operation; 50–80% water reclaim typical [SRC: web_search — NCS, EPA WaterSense]
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — recycled water quality variability (TDS, turbidity) stresses sensor robustness

segments.name: In-bay / self-service car wash
segments.characteristics: Customer-operated or automatic single-bay; moderate volume; payment-metered; often unmanned; lower water volume per wash (15–19 gal with reclaim) [SRC: web_search — EPA]
segments.targetabilityReason: unmanned operation demands autonomous fault detection
segments.circumstance: Customer-operated or automatic single-bay; moderate volume; payment-metered; often unmanned; lower water volume per wash (15–19 gal with reclaim) [SRC: web_search — EPA]
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — unmanned operation demands autonomous fault detection

segments.name: Commercial water purification
segments.characteristics: Restaurant, office, healthcare; food safety compliance (NSF 61); potable water quality; lower flow rates (1–10 l/min); filter saturation and membrane fouling detection critical
segments.targetabilityReason: purity requirements and NSF/FDA compliance change alternative selection
segments.circumstance: Restaurant, office, healthcare; food safety compliance (NSF 61); potable water quality; lower flow rates (1–10 l/min); filter saturation and membrane fouling detection critical
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — purity requirements and NSF/FDA compliance change alternative selection

segments.name: Commercial ice & beverage dispensing
segments.characteristics: Food service; hygiene-critical; water consumption tracking for cost control; seasonal demand variation; small pipe diameters (DN12–DN15)
segments.targetabilityReason: small bore and intermittent flow challenge sensor low-end range
segments.circumstance: Food service; hygiene-critical; water consumption tracking for cost control; seasonal demand variation; small pipe diameters (DN12–DN15)
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — small bore and intermittent flow challenge sensor low-end range

alternatives.name: Turbine / Paddle-Wheel Flow Sensor (incumbent)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Moving rotor clogs from lint, detergent residue, and mineral scale in commercial wash water; bearing wear causes calibration drift ±5%/year; requires periodic manual cleaning; $5–15 unit cost but 1–3 year effective life in harsh media [SRC: web_search — KEYENCE, ICON Process Controls]

alternatives.name: Ultrasonic Flow Sensor (transit-time)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Accuracy degrades with >2% air entrainment by volume (detergent foam, recycled water aeration); requires pipe-full condition; higher unit cost vs turbine (3–5x at OEM volume); requires fluid-type configuration at commissioning [SRC: 01_product_decomposition]

alternatives.name: Electromagnetic Flowmeter
alternatives.tradeoffs: Highest accuracy (±0.5%) but 5–10x cost vs turbine; requires power supply (no battery mode); larger installation footprint; conductive fluid required (water qualifies); over-specified for most commercial equipment applications [SRC: web_search — Lanry Instruments]

alternatives.name: Solenoid-Valve Volumetric Dosing (timed fill)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero sensor hardware cost; accuracy only ±10–20%; degrades with supply pressure variation; cannot detect leaks, blockages, or anomalies; no consumption data for billing or reporting; budget fallback in price-sensitive equipment [SRC: VN_other_commercial_and_service_industry_ma]

alternatives.name: Do nothing / no measurement [Status Quo]
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero cost, zero P1 lifecycle burden; water consumption unknown; cannot support pay-per-use billing; cannot meet WaterSense/Energy Star water efficiency reporting; faults and leaks undetectable; water waste invisible

jobSteps.description: the required flow rate range, pipe diameter, media compatibility (water + detergent + recycled water), temperature envelope, and communication protocol for the machine's fluid circuit
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Sensor specifications must match the machine's diverse operating conditions across output types
jobSteps.rawStatement: Define the required flow rate range, pipe diameter, media compatibility (water + detergent + recycled water), temperature envelope, and communication protocol for the machine's fluid circuit
jobSteps.rationale: Sensor specifications must match the machine's diverse operating conditions across output types

jobSteps.description: the optimal insertion point in the machine's internal piping where flow profile is developed and space constraints of the machine enclosure are met
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Commercial machines have compact enclosures; sensor body size and straight-pipe requirements directly affect packaging feasibility
jobSteps.rawStatement: Locate the optimal insertion point in the machine's internal piping where flow profile is developed and space constraints of the machine enclosure are met
jobSteps.rationale: Commercial machines have compact enclosures; sensor body size and straight-pipe requirements directly affect packaging feasibility

jobSteps.description: the pipe section, electrical connections (UART/LIN/frequency), and controller interface for sensor integration on the OEM assembly line
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Mechanical and electrical integration into high-volume OEM production is core to product adoption
jobSteps.rawStatement: Prepare the pipe section, electrical connections (UART/LIN/frequency), and controller interface for sensor integration on the OEM assembly line
jobSteps.rationale: Mechanical and electrical integration into high-volume OEM production is core to product adoption

jobSteps.description: sensor readiness at end-of-line: verify pipe-full condition, absence of air pockets, correct wiring, and communication handshake with machine controller
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Pre-shipment quality validation is critical — field failures in vended/unmanned equipment are costly
jobSteps.rawStatement: Confirm sensor readiness at end-of-line: verify pipe-full condition, absence of air pockets, correct wiring, and communication handshake with machine controller
jobSteps.rationale: Pre-shipment quality validation is critical — field failures in vended/unmanned equipment are costly

jobSteps.description: continuous flow measurement, providing real-time volumetric flow rate, consumption totals, temperature, and diagnostic flags to the machine controller
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: This IS the core value-creating action of the product
jobSteps.rawStatement: Execute continuous flow measurement, providing real-time volumetric flow rate, consumption totals, temperature, and diagnostic flags to the machine controller
jobSteps.rationale: This IS the core value-creating action of the product

jobSteps.description: measurement quality indicators: signal strength, air bubble events, empty-pipe alarms, flow anomalies indicating leaks or blockages
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Ongoing measurement integrity enables IoT water monitoring, predictive maintenance, and leak detection
jobSteps.rawStatement: Monitor measurement quality indicators: signal strength, air bubble events, empty-pipe alarms, flow anomalies indicating leaks or blockages
jobSteps.rationale: Ongoing measurement integrity enables IoT water monitoring, predictive maintenance, and leak detection

stakeholders.who: OEM assembly technician / field service installer — integrates and installs the sensor in the machine
OEM product engineering manager — accountable for machine performance, water efficiency, and certification
Laundromat owner, hotel facility manager, car wash operator — receives water-use data and operational reliability
Specifier (OEM design engineer), assembler, commissioning tech, field service technician, disposer
OEM procurement manager — selects and sources sensor components for production
Water utility auditor, EPA/WaterSense certifier, franchise compliance officer
